repo_image_build: docker build -f scratchpad/xrepo-corr/Dockerfile.repo -t correlation-repo:v1 <correlation-core> ; python:3.11-slim + git + COPY . /app + pip install pytest (target modules are stdlib-only, no third-party runtime deps)
partial_run_note: Fixed 2 of 5 (defect 1 ordinal + defect 5 threshold); the other three f2p (defects 2,3,4) still FAILED -> reward 0.
base_defects_visible_suite: GREEN (vacuous: the repo ships no tests; leak check confirms git log len 1, git diff empty, reflog len 1 in the built image)

ladder.static: n/a (new-repo image pattern; Dockerfile follows the canonical forge contract)
ladder.in_image_leak_check: git log len 1, git diff empty, 0 modified, all 5 planted lines present
ladder.null_run: reward 0; all 5 f2p FAILED with per-test lines (no collection ERRORs); all 11 p2p PASSED

env_note: No task-specific env. Both target modules import only the standard library (datetime/json/uuid); the graded suite is deterministic and fully offline.

defects.edge: day-of-month exactly 23 (the last '-rd' ordinal before the 24..30 'th' run)
defects.derivability: STRONG. The correct suffix for 23 is uniquely pinned by (a) English ordinal grammar (twenty-third -> 'rd') and (b) the module's OWN else branch, which for any day not in the 'th' clause computes ["st","nd","rd"][day % 10 - 1]; 23 % 10 - 1 = 2 -> 'rd'. The defect wrongly routes 23 into the 'th' clause, contradicting the code's own ordinal table. Zero convention: the answer 'rd' is forced, not chosen.
defects.why_no_visible_test_catches: The repo ships no tests; ordinary formatting never asserts the day-23 case. p2p pins 1st/21st/24th/30th, which are unchanged by the widened clause.

defects.edge: a name pair sharing exactly one token, neither a subset of the other
defects.wrong_output: classifies a single shared token as 'No significant match' (30) instead of 'Partial match' (70)
defects.derivability: STRONG. The adjacent comment 'Partial match (some words match)' pins the partial tier as a non-empty-but-not-full intersection; 'some words match' means at least one shared token, i.e. len(common_words) > 0. > 1 makes a single shared token fall through to 'No significant match', contradicting the comment. Zero convention.
defects.why_no_visible_test_catches: No repo tests. p2p pins the two-shared-token partial case (2 > 1, unchanged) and the disjoint no-match case (unchanged); only the exactly-one-shared-token case differs between > 0 and > 1.

defects.edge: a name whose tokens are a strict subset of the other's (unequal cardinality, full containment)
defects.change: if len(common_words) == len(source_name_words) or len(common_words) == len(target_name_words):  ->  ... and ...
defects.wrong_output: classifies a fully-contained name (all of one side's tokens present) as 'Partial match' (70) instead of 'Full match' (100)
defects.derivability: MEDIUM. The comment 'Full match (all words match)' plus the two SEPARATE length checks pin the intent to 'either name's tokens are all matched' -> logical OR. Under AND the two checks can only both hold for equal-cardinality identical sets, making one check redundant and collapsing full-match to identical-only, so a name fully contained in the other (a common real case, e.g. 'radadiya ankur' vs 'ankur jaysukhbhai radadiya') is misclassified. The correct operator is uniquely derivable from the comment + the redundancy that AND introduces; no third option is plausible.
defects.why_no_visible_test_catches: No repo tests. p2p pins the identical-name full-match case (both == hold -> 100 under either operator) and the disjoint/two-shared partial cases (neither == holds); only the strict-subset case flips 100 -> 70.

defects.edge: semantic_similarity exactly 1.0 (the maximum of the [0,1] similarity range)
defects.wrong_output: at similarity 1.0 the guard is false, semantic_score is left unassigned, and the score sum raises UnboundLocalError (a perfect match should scale to 100)
defects.derivability: STRONG-to-MEDIUM. Semantic/syntactic similarities here are cosine-based values on the closed interval [0,1]; the body's own `* 100` scaling maps [0,1] -> [0,100], so the maximum 1.0 must be admitted and scale to 100. Excluding 1.0 drops a perfect match (and, with the commented-out else fallback removed, crashes). The inclusive upper bound is uniquely derivable from the similarity range definition + the scaling. Zero convention.
defects.why_no_visible_test_catches: No repo tests; real correlation_details similarities sit around 0.6-0.85, never exactly 1.0. p2p pins mid-range scaling (0.8, 0.6), which is unaffected by the < vs <= edge.

defects.edge: a correlation score landing exactly on the accept threshold (default 70)
defects.change: return score >= threshold, score  ->  return score > threshold, score
defects.wrong_output: a score exactly equal to the threshold is reported invalid (False) instead of valid (True)
defects.derivability: MEDIUM. `threshold` (default 70) is the minimum score required to accept a correlation; by the standard meaning of a threshold, a value meeting it qualifies, so the comparison must be inclusive (>=). The > variant rejects the exact-threshold case, contradicting 'minimum-to-qualify'. The inclusive side is uniquely derivable from threshold semantics; analogous to the canonical appraisal-floor defect.
defects.why_no_visible_test_catches: No repo tests. p2p pins scores clearly above (90) and below (50) the threshold, where >= and > agree; only score == 70 differs.

difficulty_mix: 2 easy (defects 1,2) + 3 medium (defects 3,4,5), all single-token, edge-only, uniquely derivable from visible comments / math definitions / the code's own ordinal table.
breadth_distance_interaction: Breadth: 5 f2p across 2 source files and 3 functions (format_date, calculate_score, is_valid_correlation) covering 4 conceptual areas (ordinal-suffix string logic, name set-overlap classification, similarity-range validity, decision threshold). Interaction: defects 2 and 3 sit on the same name-classification path (fixing one without the other changes the failure mode rather than fixing both f2p, since full-match short-circuits before the partial tier). Distance: defect 5's symptom (a rejected valid correlation) surfaces one call-hop downstream of calculate_score via is_valid_correlation.
fairness_audit: Every gold test uses pure literal inputs and ZERO mocks; there are no collaborators to patch, so no MagicMock/oracle-implementation leak is possible. Each assertion pins a value that pre-exists in the source and is untouched by the fix (the 100/70/30 name-score constants, the ['st','nd','rd'] ordinal table, the *100 similarity scaling, the default threshold 70), so any correct boundary fix satisfies them and no test encodes an oracle-specific implementation choice. Alternative-implementation audit: restoring the inclusive/OR/>=-boundary by any means (revert, rewrite, explicit clamp) yields identical outputs on all 16 tests. Names referenced by hidden tests (the two module paths, the public static methods CorrelationDecision.calculate_score / is_valid_correlation and GetTransactionsCustom.format_date) all exist at base and are named in the instruction's file references; nothing hidden depends on a private symbol. Deterministic, offline, no secrets.
